conditions.vaName: Spine — General Rating Formula for Diseases and Injuries of the Spine
conditions.summary: Spine conditions (degenerative disc disease, strain, etc.) are rated primarily on range-of-motion measurements (forward flexion) and whether ankylosis (fusion/immobility) is present, with separate consideration for incapacitating episodes if the condition involves intervertebral disc disease.

ratingTiers.criteria: Forward flexion of the thoracolumbar spine greater than 60° but not greater than 85°, or combined range of motion greater than 120° but not greater than 235°
Forward flexion greater than 30° but not greater than 60°, or muscle spasm/guarding severe enough to result in abnormal gait or spinal contour
Forward flexion of 30° or less, or favorable ankylosis of the entire thoracolumbar spine
Unfavorable ankylosis of the entire thoracolumbar spine
Unfavorable ankylosis of the entire spine

conditions.summary: Knee conditions are rated based on range of motion (flexion/extension), instability, or other specific impairments like ankylosis or removal of cartilage — different diagnostic codes apply depending on which specific impairment is present.

ratingTiers.criteria: Slight limitation of motion or slight instability
Moderate limitation of motion or moderate instability
Severe limitation of motion, severe instability, or ankylosis (this and higher tiers vary by which specific diagnostic code applies)

conditions.summary: Tinnitus (ringing in the ears) is rated as a single condition regardless of whether it affects one or both ears.

ratingTiers.criteria: Recurrent tinnitus (this is the maximum schedular rating for tinnitus alone)

conditions.summary: Hearing loss is rated using a table that combines results from a controlled speech discrimination test and a pure tone audiometry test for each ear, producing a Roman numeral (I-XI) for each ear, which is then cross-referenced on a rating table.

ratingTiers.criteria: Hearing within normal limits or mild loss not meeting threshold criteria
Determined by cross-referencing both ears' test results (Table VI/VII) — not a simple percentage of hearing lost. 10%-100% tiers all use this same cross-reference method.

conditions.summary: Sleep apnea is rated primarily based on the treatment required — whether a CPAP or similar breathing assistance device is medically required is the key factor at the 50% level.

ratingTiers.criteria: Asymptomatic but with documented sleep disorder breathing
Persistent daytime hypersomnolence
Requires use of a breathing assistance device such as CPAP
Chronic respiratory failure with carbon dioxide retention or cor pulmonale, or requires tracheostomy

conditions.vaName: Hypertensive Vascular Disease (Hypertension and Isolated Systolic Hypertension)
conditions.todo: Populate ratingTiers from eCFR.gov — verify DC 7101 tier language (currently believed to be 10/20/40/60%, diastolic/systolic thresholds) against the current regulation text before marking verified.

conditions.summary: GERD and similar conditions are rated based on the severity and frequency of symptoms like pain, regurgitation, dysphagia (difficulty swallowing), and their effect on health and weight.

ratingTiers.criteria: Two or more symptoms for the 30% rating but of lesser severity
Persistently recurrent epigastric distress with dysphagia, pyrosis, and regurgitation, accompanied by substernal or arm/shoulder pain, productive of considerable impairment of health
Symptoms of pain, vomiting, material weight loss, and hematemesis or melena with moderate anemia, or other symptom combinations productive of severe impairment of health

bodySystems.name: Gynecological Conditions & Disorders of the Breast

conditions.summary: Scars are rated based on location (head/face/neck vs. elsewhere), size (area in square inches/cm), and whether they are painful, unstable, or cause limitation of motion.

ratingTiers.criteria: Scar(s) that are not painful or unstable and do not limit motion, below the size threshold
One or two scars that are painful or unstable, or scars meeting minimum size thresholds
Multiple scars meeting size/severity thresholds, or scars causing limitation of motion rated under the affected body part (this and the 30%+ tier vary by exact diagnostic code)

conditions.todo: Populate ratingTiers from eCFR.gov — verify DC 7913 tier language (currently believed to be 10/20/40/60/100%, based on insulin/diet/activity-restriction criteria) against current regulation text before marking verified. Type 2 diabetes is a long-standing Agent Orange presumptive, reaffirmed/expanded under PACT Act — double-check current presumptive exposure criteria, not just the rating tiers, since those rules move faster than the base schedule.

conditions.summary: Migraines are rated based on the frequency and severity of "prostrating attacks" — attacks severe enough that the person must stop activity and seek a dark, quiet place.

ratingTiers.criteria: Less frequent attacks
Characteristic prostrating attacks averaging one in 2 months over the last several months
Characteristic prostrating attacks occurring on average once a month over the last several months
Very frequent completely prostrating and prolonged attacks productive of severe economic inadaptability

conditions.summary: Nerve conditions (often associated with spine conditions, causing pain/numbness/weakness radiating into an arm or leg) are rated based on whether the nerve impairment is "mild," "moderate," "moderately severe," or "severe," and whether it is "incomplete" or "complete" paralysis — and which specific nerve is affected.

ratingTiers.criteria: Mild incomplete paralysis of the affected nerve
Moderate incomplete paralysis
Moderately severe to severe incomplete paralysis (exact % depends on which nerve and which limb)
Complete paralysis of the nerve — exact % depends on which specific nerve and limb is affected, can be higher than 40% for some nerves

conditions.todo: Populate facets from eCFR.gov — DC 8045 scores 10 facets of function (memory/concentration, judgment, social interaction, motor activity, visual-spatial, subjective symptoms, neurobehavioral, communication, consciousness, plus a glasgow coma scale facet), each at level 0-3 or "total", with the highest facet score across all of them determining the overall rating. ratingTiers intentionally stays [] per ratingMethod: "faceted" — do not populate it for this condition.

conditions.summary: Mental disorders are rated under a "General Rating Formula" based on the degree of occupational and social impairment caused by symptoms — not by diagnosis name alone. The same formula applies to PTSD, depression, anxiety, and most other mental health conditions.

ratingTiers.criteria: Diagnosis present, symptoms not severe enough to interfere with work or social function, or symptoms controlled by medication
Occasional decrease in work efficiency, intermittent periods of inability to perform tasks (though generally functioning satisfactorily)
Reduced reliability and productivity due to symptoms
Deficiencies in most areas (work, school, family relations, judgment, thinking, mood)
Total occupational and social impairment

bodySystems.name: Infectious Diseases, Immune Disorders & Nutritional Deficiencies
